title: MultisensoryFlickerHealthyYoungAdults_AllSubjectsRawData
description: This dataset contains scalp EEG recordings from healthy young adults (age range not specified) exposed to audiovisual flicker stimulation at 40 Hz, control (no flicker), or randomized flicker conditions during a one-hour psychomotor vigilance task. The study investigates neural responses to multisensory flicker stimulation and its effects on vigilance performance.
methods_description: Scalp EEG activity was recorded during one-hour sessions of either 40 Hz audiovisual flicker, no flicker control, or randomized flicker sham stimulation while subjects performed a psychomotor vigilance task.
